symptoms.symptom_name: Cold-triggered triphasic digital colour change (white to blue to red)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic Raynaud vasospasm is a core feature of connective-tissue autoimmunity/UCTD.
Objective vasospastic colour change is not a feature of fibromyalgia, which produces subjective pain without vasospasm.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical inflammatory polyarthralgia with subjective wrist/knee swelling and stiffness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory, non-erosive polyarthralgia is a common connective-tissue-disease feature accompanying a flare.
Fatigue is a non-specific but characteristic accompaniment of an autoimmune flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Digits reperfusing (colour returning, warming) with no persistently white or blackening digit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Active reperfusion with no fixed pallor is inconsistent with a persistently occluded artery.
Reversible, self-resolving vasospasm is the expected behaviour of a Raynaud attack.
Embolic occlusion is typically a single, unilateral, persistently ischaemic limb/digit, not symmetrical simultaneous involvement.
Symmetrical bilateral digital vasospasm is typical of primary/secondary Raynaud.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive antinuclear antibody (ANA) with clinical autoimmune features not meeting criteria for a defined CTD

investigation_details.investigation_name: Antinuclear antibody (ANA) and connective-tissue autoantibody panel
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive ANA (already known to the patient); further autoantibody profile and titre pending — supports a connective-tissue autoimmune process but is non-diagnostic of a single named disease
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without serology and specialist assessment the connective-tissue process cannot be characterised or progression monitored.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positive ANA with autoimmune clinical features is the serological basis of the UCTD label.

symptoms.symptom_name: Radial pulses strong and equal bilaterally

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact, equal radial pulses make a major proximal arterial occlusion very unlikely — but this is a clinician palpation finding the AI cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of sclerodactyly, skin tightening and telangiectasia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of skin thickening/telangiectasia argues against systemic sclerosis, though it does not exclude early disease — an examination finding not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Functional impairment: unable to use hands to prepare food or manage medications, living alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marks flare severity and social risk (safety-netting feasibility) rather than discriminating the diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No digital skin ulceration, blistering or gangrene

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of ulceration/gangrene supports an uncomplicated (non-critical) Raynaud flare; its presence would escalate to urgent vascular assessment.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Bilateral symmetrical simultaneous involvement + active reperfusion + intact pulses together make acute arterial occlusion very unlikely — the constellation, not any single feature, is what safely downgrades the vascular emergency.
Raynaud + inflammatory polyarthralgia + fatigue + positive ANA together constitute the UCTD flare picture, none of which is individually diagnostic.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Connective-tissue autoantibody panel (ANA titre, ENA, complement) and inflammatory markers via GP/rheumatology
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises the autoimmune process and monitors for evolution toward a defined CTD — arranged through GP/rheumatology, not urgently in this consult.
Not indicated for a bilateral, symmetrical, reperfusing Raynaud flare with intact pulses; reserved for a single persistently pulseless cold limb (suspected arterial occlusion).
